what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, pointing to an icon asset named Virtual_Hub.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no prose body.
standouts: Target uses an image:// URI scheme rather than a filesystem path.
The added timestamp is dated in 2026.
No file extension or version is specified for the image asset.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Attachment ids use an att- prefix.
Records channel, attachment type, target asset URI, and added timestamp.
concerns: The target URI omits file extension, dimensions, and MIME type.
The future timestamp may indicate synthetic or test data.
There is no alt text, description, or uploader information.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that links the design-assets channel to the Virtual_Hub icon image asset.
questions: What image format does Virtual_Hub use?
How is the image:// URI resolved to an actual file or storage location?
Why is the added timestamp set in 2026?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it records an image attachment reference for a channel.
intention_signals: To create a discoverable, timestamped ledger entry associating a channel with a specific image asset.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme for the target and a channel-specific attachment ledger entry.
contribution: Provides a canonical reference record that maps a channel to an image asset, supporting retrieval and audit of design assets.
summary_for_embedding: YAML attachment ledger entry att-fa9f7087 in the design-assets channel referencing the image://design-assets/icons/Virtual_Hub asset with a 2026 timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record and expose the association between the design-assets channel and the Virtual_Hub icon image asset.
